task_completion.description: Investigate security alerts using the Wazuh dashboard or API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Wazuh dashboard open in a browser, or terminal commands (like curl) querying the Wazuh indexer (port 9200) or manager API for alerts matching the wazuh-alerts-4.x-2025.03.* pattern.

task_completion.description: Save the investigation findings to ~/apt_report.json with all required keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the creation or contents of ~/apt_report.json. The JSON must contain the exact keys: attacker_ip, compromised_account, compromised_host, lateral_movement_target, privilege_escalation_method, persistence_mechanism, and attack_timeline.

task_completion.description: Create correlated detection rules in the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container using if_matched_sid
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing interaction with the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container (e.g., via docker exec) and editing /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. The added rules must clearly show the use of the <if_matched_sid> XML tag to chain stages.
Terminal showing a command executed inside the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container to restart the manager service (e.g., /var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart).

task_completion.description: Confirm the rules load successfully by querying the manager API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a curl command or API client querying the Wazuh Manager API (https://localhost:55000) to retrieve or validate the newly created rules, along with a successful response.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually investigated the alerts rather than hallucinating the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual search results, log entries, or API responses from Wazuh on screen before the apt_report.json is generated.

integrity.description: Verify rule creation and restart occurred inside the actual container
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' commands or a shell prompt indicating the agent is inside the container, rather than just editing local files on the host.

integrity.description: Verify the API verification was a genuine network request
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a real curl command or script execution targeting port 55000 with proper authentication, returning a standard Wazuh API JSON response, rather than a manually typed mock response.
